title: Fly Me to the Moon
wikitext:
'''Fly Me to the Moon''' was chosen as the ending theme for the Evangelion TV series. The music and lyrics were composed by [[Bart Howard]], originally released under the title "In Other Words," with the title being officially changed to "Fly Me to the Moon" a few years later. It had already been covered and released countless times by various artists, most notably by Frank Sinatra. ==Lyrics== ''Fly me to the moon
''And let me play among the stars
''Let me see what spring is like
''On Jupiter and Mars
''In other words, hold my hand
''In other words, darling kiss me
''Fill my heart with song
''And let me sing forevermore
''You are all I long for
''All I worship and adore
''In other words, please be true
''In other words, I love you''
==The Intro== ''Fly Me to the Moon'' has an intro that is omitted by most artists, including Sinatra (Perry Como included it in his version): ''Poets often use many words to say a simple thing.
''It takes thought and rhyme to make a poem sing.
''With words and with music I've been playing.
''For you I have written this song.
''To make sure you understand what I'm saying.
''I'll translate as I go along…''
==Versions for Evangelion== The pre-Renewal releases of Evangelion (the original televised — or OA — version and LD/video version) had multiple versions of this song that it would use for the end credits of different episodes. However, for the Renewal DVD release, even ''more'' new versions were created: some edits of versions previously released to album, others completely new (the vocal tracks, anyway). In the Renewal, every episode has a unique version of FMTTM for its ED (or, at least, was ''supposed'' to… see the endnotes). It's quite a mess, so here's a concise guide to exactly which versions of FMTTM are used in both the original and Renewal versions of the series. Hopefully, it will be the final word on the matter. “Release Title” is how that particular version of FMTTM was entitled for album release. “Version” is how a particular version of FMTTM is designated in [[Data of Evangelion]]. {| cellspacing="0" border="1" |- ! Episode ! Release Title ! Version ! ² | Trio Vocal Interlude Start ~ #1 Second Half/NORMAL Orchestra² | RoE |- | #17 | FLY ME TO THE MOON | Aki Jungle | TV-III |- | #18 (Original) | FLY ME TO THE MOON | NORMAL/CLAIRE | TV-II |- | #18 (Renewal) | B-4 guitar & piano | B-4 guitar & piano | S² Works |- | #19 (Original) | FLY ME TO THE MOON | NORMAL/CLAIRE | TV-II |- | #19 (Renewal) | na | Trio Vocal (Singing Shared)/NORMAL Orchestra² | na |- | #20 (Original) | FLY ME TO THE MOON | na | TV-III |- | #20 (Renewal) | B-4 piano | B-4 piano | S² Works |- | #21 (Original: OA & NPC, Renewal: OA) | FLY ME TO THE MOON | NORMAL/YOKO TAKAHASHI⁴ | TV-III |- | #21 (Renewal: NPC) | na | 4BEAT (OFF) (90-seconds) | na |- | #22 (Original: OA & NPC, Renewal: OA) | FLY ME TO THE MOON | Aya Bossa Techno⁵ | TV-III |- | #22 (Renewal: NPC) | na | Bossa Techno (OFF) (80-seconds) | na |- | #23 (Original: OA & NPC, Renewal: OA) | FLY ME TO THE MOON | Rei#23 (65-seconds) | TV-II |- | #23 (Renewal: NPC) | na | Rei#23 (90-seconds) | na |- | #24 (Original: OA & NPC, Renewal: OA) | FLY ME TO THE MOON | NORMAL (OFF) (65-seconds) | TV-III |- | #24 (Renewal: NPC) | na | NORMAL (OFF) (90-seconds) | na |- | #25 | FLY ME TO THE MOON | Rei#25 | TV-III |- | #26 | FLY ME TO THE MOON | Rei#26 | TV-III |- | DVD Renewal ED (DISC 1~8) | na | B-4 piano (48-seconds) | na |- | DVD Renewal ED (DISC 9) | na | B-4 piano (43-seconds) | na |} *1. This is a shortened version of “FLY ME TO THE MOON ” from ADDITION, although, unlike that release, this one actually is TV size. *2. It seems these are all TV-sized edits of “FLY ME TO THE MOON
” from ADDITION. *3. This is a shortened version of “FLY ME TO THE MOON —MISATO 4 BEAT TV.Size VERSION—” from ADDITION, although, unlike that release, this one actually is TV size. *4. Data of Evangelion (an information booklet which came with the Renewal DVD-BOX) lists this version as “4BEAT (OFF) (65-seconds)”, but this isn't what is actually played (if an audio rip somebody with Renewal provided me is reliable, anyway). *5.Similar problem as above. Listed in Data as “Bossa Techno (OFF) (65-seconds)”, but that's not the version that plays. ==Evangelion 1.0== Fly Me to the Moon was covered by Utada Hikaru for use in trailers for [[Evangelion 1.0: You Are (Not) Alone]]. It was included as the B-side track on Hikaru's single, [[Beautiful World]]/Kiss and Cry, titled "Fly Me to the Moon (In Other Words) -2007 MIX-." It was never released for any sort of airplay, save for TV commercials promoting Rebuild of Evangelion. ==Notes== *There are 31 different versions of this song for the original series. *Information about the singer Claire can be found here: [[Claire]] {{Audio Releases}} [[Category:A to Z]] [[Category:Theme Songs]]
html:
Fly Me to the Moon was chosen as the ending theme for the Evangelion TV series. The music and lyrics were composed by Bart Howard, originally released under the title "In Other Words," with the title being officially changed to "Fly Me to the Moon" a few years later. It had already been covered and released countless times by various artists, most notably by Frank Sinatra.

Contents

  • 1 Lyrics
  • 2 The Intro
  • 3 Versions for Evangelion
  • 4 Evangelion 1.0
  • 5 Notes

Lyrics[edit]

Fly me to the moon
 And let me play among the stars
 Let me see what spring is like
 On Jupiter and Mars
 In other words, hold my hand
 In other words, darling kiss me

Fill my heart with song
 And let me sing forevermore
 You are all I long for
 All I worship and adore
 In other words, please be true
 In other words, I love you

The Intro[edit]

Fly Me to the Moon has an intro that is omitted by most artists, including Sinatra (Perry Como included it in his version):

Poets often use many words to say a simple thing.
 It takes thought and rhyme to make a poem sing.
 With words and with music I've been playing.
 For you I have written this song.
 To make sure you understand what I'm saying.
 I'll translate as I go along…

Versions for Evangelion[edit]

The pre-Renewal releases of Evangelion (the original televised — or OA — version and LD/video version) had multiple versions of this song that it would use for the end credits of different episodes. However, for the Renewal DVD release, even more new versions were created: some edits of versions previously released to album, others completely new (the vocal tracks, anyway). In the Renewal, every episode has a unique version of FMTTM for its ED (or, at least, was supposed to… see the endnotes). It's quite a mess, so here's a concise guide to exactly which versions of FMTTM are used in both the original and Renewal versions of the series. Hopefully, it will be the final word on the matter.

“Release Title” is how that particular version of FMTTM was entitled for album release. “Version” is how a particular version of FMTTM is designated in Data of Evangelion.

Episode Release Title Version Release
#01 FLY ME TO THE MOON <TV.Size Version> NORMAL/CLAIRE TV-II
#02 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON <TV.Size Version> NORMAL/CLAIRE TV-II
#02 (Renewal) na NORMAL (Minus Strings)/CLAIRE na
#03 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON <TV.Size Version> NORMAL/CLAIRE TV-II
#03 (Renewal) FLY ME TO THE MOON <YOKO TAKAHASHI TV.Size Version> NORMAL/YOKO TAKAHASHI TV-III
#04 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON <TV.Size Version> NORMAL/CLAIRE TV-II
#04 (Renewal) na NORMAL (Minus Strings)/YOKO TAKAHASHI na
#05 FLY ME TO THE MOON <Rei(#5) TV.Size Remix Version> Rei#5 TV-II
#06 FLY ME TO THE MOON <Rei(#6) TV.Size Remix Version> Rei#6 TV-II
#07 FLY ME TO THE MOON —4 BEAT TV.Size VERSION— 4BEAT/YOKO TAKAHASHI TV-III
#08 FLY ME TO THE MOON <Aya Bossa Techno TV.Size Version><aya Bossa Techno TV.Size Version> Aya Bossa Techno TV-III
#09 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON <YOKO TAKAHASHI Acid Bossa TV.Size Version> YOKO TAKAHASHI Acid Bossa TV-III
#09 (Renewal) na Asuka Bossa Techno¹ na
#10 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON <YOKO TAKAHASHI TV.Size Version> NORMAL/YOKO TAKAHASHI TV-III
#10 (Renewal) na Asuka Main (Entering Chorus)/NORMAL Orchestra na
#11 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON <TV.Size Version> NORMAL/CLAIRE TV-II
#11 (Renewal) na Rei, Asuka, Misato Ver./NORMAL Orchestra² na
#12 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON —4 BEAT TV.Size VERSION— 4BEAT/YOKO TAKAHASHI TV-III
#12 (Renewal) na Misato Main (Entering Chorus)/NORMAL Orchestra na
#13 FLY ME TO THE MOON <YOKO TAKAHASHI Acid Bossa TV.Size Version> YOKO TAKAHASHI Acid Bossa TV-III
#14 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON <YOKO TAKAHASHI TV.Size Version> NORMAL/YOKO TAKAHASHI TV-III
#14 (Renewal) na Rei Solo/NORMAL Orchestra na
#15 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON —4 BEAT OFF VOCAL TV.Size VERSION— 4BEAT (OFF) (65-seconds) TV-III
#15 (Renewal) na Misato 4BEAT³ na
#16 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON <OFF VOCAL TV.Size Version> NORMAL (OFF) (65-seconds) TV-III
#16 (Renewal) FLY ME TO THE MOON <Main Version II/Renewal #16>² Trio Vocal Interlude Start ~ #1 Second Half/NORMAL Orchestra² RoE
#17 FLY ME TO THE MOON <Aki Jungle TV.Size Version> Aki Jungle TV-III
#18 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON <TV.Size Version> NORMAL/CLAIRE TV-II
#18 (Renewal) B-4 guitar & piano B-4 guitar & piano S² Works
#19 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON <TV.Size Version> NORMAL/CLAIRE TV-II
#19 (Renewal) na Trio Vocal (Singing Shared)/NORMAL Orchestra² na
#20 (Original) FLY ME TO THE MOON <B-22A TV.Size Version> na TV-III
#20 (Renewal) B-4 piano B-4 piano S² Works
#21 (Original: OA & NPC, Renewal: OA) FLY ME TO THE MOON <YOKO TAKAHASHI TV.Size Version> NORMAL/YOKO TAKAHASHI⁴ TV-III
#21 (Renewal: NPC) na 4BEAT (OFF) (90-seconds) na
#22 (Original: OA & NPC, Renewal: OA) FLY ME TO THE MOON <Aya Bossa Techno TV.Size Version> Aya Bossa Techno⁵ TV-III
#22 (Renewal: NPC) na Bossa Techno (OFF) (80-seconds) na
#23 (Original: OA & NPC, Renewal: OA) FLY ME TO THE MOON <Rei(#23) TV.Size Version> Rei#23 (65-seconds) TV-II
#23 (Renewal: NPC) na Rei#23 (90-seconds) na
#24 (Original: OA & NPC, Renewal: OA) FLY ME TO THE MOON <OFF VOCAL TV.Size Version> NORMAL (OFF) (65-seconds) TV-III
#24 (Renewal: NPC) na NORMAL (OFF) (90-seconds) na
#25 FLY ME TO THE MOON <Rei(#25) TV.Size Version> Rei#25 TV-III
#26 FLY ME TO THE MOON <Rei(#26) TV.Size Version> Rei#26 TV-III
DVD Renewal ED (DISC 1~8) na B-4 piano (48-seconds) na
DVD Renewal ED (DISC 9) na B-4 piano (43-seconds) na
  • 1. This is a shortened version of “FLY ME TO THE MOON <Asuka Bossa Techno TV.Size Version>” from ADDITION, although, unlike that release, this one actually is TV size.
  • 2. It seems these are all TV-sized edits of “FLY ME TO THE MOON <Main Version II>” from ADDITION.
  • 3. This is a shortened version of “FLY ME TO THE MOON —MISATO 4 BEAT TV.Size VERSION—” from ADDITION, although, unlike that release, this one actually is TV size.
  • 4. Data of Evangelion (an information booklet which came with the Renewal DVD-BOX) lists this version as “4BEAT (OFF) (65-seconds)”, but this isn't what is actually played (if an audio rip somebody with Renewal provided me is reliable, anyway).
  • 5.Similar problem as above. Listed in Data as “Bossa Techno (OFF) (65-seconds)”, but that's not the version that plays.

Evangelion 1.0[edit]

Fly Me to the Moon was covered by Utada Hikaru for use in trailers for Evangelion 1.0: You Are (Not) Alone. It was included as the B-side track on Hikaru's single, Beautiful World/Kiss and Cry, titled "Fly Me to the Moon (In Other Words) -2007 MIX-." It was never released for any sort of airplay, save for TV commercials promoting Rebuild of Evangelion.

Notes[edit]

  • There are 31 different versions of this song for the original series.
  • Information about the singer Claire can be found here: Claire
Audio Releases
Arranged Albums: Evangelion Symphony | Evangelion Vox | Evangelion Wind Symphony | Evangelion PianoForte #1 | The world! Evangelion Jazz night | Outtakes from Evangelion (Vol.1) | Shin Godzilla vs. Evangelion Symphony
Compilation Albums: The Day of Second Impact | Evangelion: The Birthday of Rei Ayanami | Refrain of Evangelion | Evangelion: Decade | Neon Genesis Evangelion Soundtrack 25th Anniversary Box | Evangelion Finally
Soundtrack Albums: Neon Genesis Evangelion OST I | Neon Genesis Evangelion OST II | Neon Genesis Evangelion OST III | Evangelion Death | End of Evangelion | S² Works | Music from Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone | Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone Original Soundtrack | Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance Original Soundtrack | Music from Evangelion: 3.0 You Can (Not) Redo | Evangelion: 3.0 You Can (Not) Redo Original Soundtrack | Music from "Shin Evangelion" Evangelion: 3.0+1.0
Studio Albums: Neon Genesis Evangelion Addition | Neon Genesis Evangelion Classical Volume | Refrain, The Songs Were Inspired by Evangelion | Evangelion Extreme
Theme Songs: A Cruel Angel's Thesis | Fly Me to the Moon | Soul's Refrain | Thanatos -If I can't be yours- | Komm, süsser Tod | Everything You've Ever Dreamed | Beautiful World | Sakura Nagashi | One Last Kiss
Other: After the End | Animate Voice Cassette
summary: Fly Me to the Moon was chosen as the ending theme for the Evangelion TV series. The music and lyrics were composed by Bart Howard, originally released under the title "In Other Words," with the title being officially changed to "Fly Me to the Moon" a few years later. It had already been covered and released countless times by various artists, most notably by Frank Sinatra.

Fly Me to the Moon was chosen as the ending theme for the Evangelion TV series. The music and lyrics were composed by Bart Howard, originally released under the title "In Other Words," with the title being officially changed to "Fly Me to the Moon" a few years later. It had already been covered and released countless times by various artists, most notably by Frank Sinatra.

Lyrics
Fly me to the moonAnd let me play among the stars
Let me see what spring is likeOn Jupiter and Mars
In other words, hold my handIn other words, darling kiss me 

Fill my heart with songAnd let me sing forevermore
You are all I long forAll I worship and adore
In other words, please be trueIn other words, I love youThe IntroFly Me to the Moon has an intro that is omitted by most artists, including Sinatra (Perry Como included it in his version):Poets often use many words to say a simple thing.
It takes thought and rhyme to make a poem sing.With words and with music I've been playing.
For you I have written this song.To make sure you understand what I'm saying.
I'll translate as I go along…

Evangelion 1.0

Fly Me to the Moon was covered by Utada Hikaru for use in trailers for Evangelion 1.0: You Are (Not) Alone. It was included as the B-side track on Hikaru's single, Beautiful World/Kiss and Cry, titled "Fly Me to the Moon (In Other Words) -2007 MIX-." It was never released for any sort of airplay, save for TV commercials promoting Rebuild of Evangelion.

Notes
There are 31 different versions of this song for the original series.
Information about the singer Claire can be found here: Claire
